name: Tender Broccoli Stems with Clams
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Canned baby clams (or crabmeat or shrimp)
2 tbsp Peanut oil for first stir-frying
recipeInstructions:
Preparation: Wash broccoli and cut off flowerets (save for another dish). With a small paring knife, carefully peel the outer skin off broccoli stems, beginning at the base. Leave inner stems whole. Cut stems on the bias into 1/4-inch slices. Rinse fermented soy beans. Peel garlic. Mince together soybeans and garlic; then mash to a paste using a mortar and pestle; set aside. Place drained baby clams in stock. If substituting fresh clams, crabmeat or shrimp, wash, shell, and chop into small pieces.
Stir-frying:
1. Heat wok to hot, then add the first of oil.
2. When oil just begins to smoke, add broccoli and toss quickly to avoid burning. Stir-fry for about 1 minute.
3. Turn heat down to moderate (or remove from heat on an electric range). Push broccoli up against the side of the wok; add remaining oil to the bottom of the wok; add soybean mixture.
4. Press and stir until the odor of beans and garlic is present.
5. Return to high heat and immediately recombine with broccoli, stirring constantly for about 30 seconds. Beans and garlic will burn easily, so keep the mixture moving.
6. Quickly add clams and stock; stir-fry for another 30 seconds.
7. Cover for 30 seconds to finish cooking broccoli. Remove cover; thicken with cornstarch paste to a light sauce.
